plan.original_text: Are CALM's operating margins compressing despite revenue growth over the past two quarters?
plan.thesis: Cal-Maine should outperform over the next 2–4 quarters if shell egg pricing remains above historical mid-cycle levels. Earnings are highly sensitive to spot egg prices due to largely fixed production costs, creating operating leverage. The thesis fails if egg prices revert to pre-cycle averages faster than expected.

steps.description: CALM quarterly operating income history — fundamental.history.operating_income
CALM derived quarterly operating margin from operating income divided by revenue
CALM operating margin trend over the past two quarters
CALM revenue quarter-over-quarter growth over the past two quarters
CALM revenue growth trend over the past two quarters
Assess whether CALM operating margins are compressing despite revenue growth over the past two quarters

ambiguity_notes.operating margins: grounded as a derived quarterly operating margin timeseries using fundamental.history.operating_income divided by fundamental.history.revenue, because only a snapshot operating_margin series was directly available and not a historical margin timeseries.
ambiguity_notes.revenue growth: grounded as quarter-over-quarter growth computed from fundamental.history.revenue over recent quarterly history.
ambiguity_notes.past two quarters: interpreted as using recent quarterly history sufficient to evaluate the latest two-quarter directional pattern; fetched 4 quarters to support margin derivation and qoq growth computation.

plan.reasoning: The question is a composite of two linked conditions: whether operating margins are compressing and whether revenue is still growing. Since no historical operating margin series was directly available, the plan derives margin from quarterly operating income and revenue, then evaluates margin direction and revenue growth before making a terminal qualitative assessment.
